Momentum, Consolidation, and Breakout
I have to be honest with you, 2023 was the hardest year of my life.=C2=A0
It wasn=E2=80=99t a bad year. It was a great year in a lot of ways. But it =
was hard.=C2=A0
The best way to describe it might be with a trading concept: consolidation.
After a big run up in the price of an asset, the momentum runs out and the =
price churns in a range for a while before breaking out or breaking down.=
=C2=A0
The first three years of Not Boring were a magical, momentum-filled run. It=
 felt like a dream. I captured some of my disbelief at my good fortune on t=
he newsletter=E2=80=99s one year birthday in A Not Boring Adventure, One Ye=
ar In [ https://substack.com/redirect/535dbe30-3170-42f2-bd40-759964fcd15e?=
j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ].
In early 2020, I=E2=80=99d quit my job at Breather, which would end up gett=
ing sold for pennies during COVID, thought about starting something with ph=
ysical spaces, which got killed by COVID, and found myself unemployed, unin=
sured with a case of COVID, with a kid on the way. So I started writing a f=
ree newsletter full time.=C2=A0
Somehow, over the next few years, the newsletter grew way bigger than I tho=
ught it could, I got to meet and become friends with founders and investors=

fund thesis. I wrote pieces on Varda [ https://substack.com/redirect/5b0c52=
13-583f-4dcd-b403-28275607db88?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjml=
XXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ] and Atomic AI [ https://substack.com/redirect/9eea=
68a9-4961-4d87-aab3-fb529ddb4f2e?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZj=
mlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ] (with Elliot), Wander [ https://substack.com/red=
irect/b68933e9-b3ad-42de-a0b5-29822cdaa4a2?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7Gn=
A_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ], Array Labs [ https://substack.com/red=
irect/1c203d9e-caa1-422e-966e-18fee1f9690a?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7Gn=
A_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ], Anduril [ https://substack.com/redire=
ct/71a9a7bb-a8f9-485c-9599-d34a4566639e?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_Z=
lNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ], LayerZero [ https://open.substack.com/pu=
b/notboring/p/layerzero-the-language-of-the-omnnichain?r=3D1ftlg&utm_campai=
gn=3Dpost&utm_medium=3Dweb&showWelcome=3Dtrue ], Ezra [ https://substack.co=
m/redirect/ead7201f-c26f-4e6a-9135-97e437245588?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J=
5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ], and The Browser Company [ https:=
//substack.com/redirect/a43adbfe-8a3a-48d6-9861-85219d06f0c7?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOX=
AwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ]. Besides Anduril and=
 LayerZero, which are already there, there are at least a couple billion do=
llar-plus companies in that group. I want to do more Deep Dives in 2024, pa=
rticularly on our portfolio companies, and become the best in the world at =
telling the stories of complex, ambitious tech startups.
Not Boring Capital. Despite sluggish fundraising, we=E2=80=99ve managed to =
build a portfolio of pretty fantastic companies, in Fund III and prior fund=
s.=C2=A0
As you read this, Not Boring Capital portfolio companies are building found=
ation models for RNA [ https://substack.com/redirect/45af188f-8d19-4f13-99a=
5-2efb4a38a214?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8E=
xkRNpU ] and for gene interactions (=F0=9F=91=80), creating physics-based A=
I chips [ https://substack.com/redirect/973a01ae-1cb4-4416-b9e1-4671d05bd52=
5?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ], bui=
lding hyperlogistics networks under cities [ https://substack.com/redirect/=
d6865d49-c8ea-4f81-a66d-03395c0ba548?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNu=
aMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ], manufacturing drugs in space [ https://subs=
tack.com/redirect/2160e032-f399-4f1e-8388-c7ede7ab98e1?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3Qi=
fQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ], accelerating manufacturin=
g to make parts for space [ https://substack.com/redirect/48617f23-cc5d-4de=
0-82e3-d39d61388aa1?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8=
-6m8ExkRNpU ], growing cabbages that produce GLP-1 [ https://substack.com/r=
edirect/511ac312-4dd7-424b-ba39-0df3e168cd8b?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7=
GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ], crafting Von Neumann universal cons=
tructors [ https://substack.com/redirect/2d204c59-1826-4d92-8eb5-4575dd220d=
54?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ], re=
building the school system [ https://substack.com/redirect/d3953699-5aac-4b=
02-b2dd-6fca80d19b74?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa=
8-6m8ExkRNpU ] and giving parents choice in their kids=E2=80=99 education [=
 https://substack.com/redirect/02bc506c-ae43-4ff5-ad4f-62b6a487ced7?j=3DeyJ=
1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ], decentralizi=
ng ID to build a more human economic system [ https://substack.com/redirect=
/912deadb-d007-4579-83a3-8bde9667cd97?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlN=
uaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ], bringing abundant energy to Africa [ https=
://substack.com/redirect/abcc2a25-16e4-413b-8e03-079af1c5be7f?j=3DeyJ1IjoiO=
XAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ], infusing AI in cod=
e [ https://substack.com/redirect/22163b0e-1faf-473b-9e9f-c2dbe8066eef?j=3D=
eyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ], bringing =
real world assets onchain [ https://substack.com/redirect/d634007c-a368-45e=
8-a62f-016d8d95f079?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8=
-6m8ExkRNpU ], creating virtual worlds [ https://substack.com/redirect/461a=
0810-821b-46be-b080-9950e65817f9?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZj=
mlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ], reimagining how the physical one gets built [ h=
ttps://substack.com/redirect/0c8ee7e8-ee50-4579-bbd6-1614e699b4fc?j=3DeyJ1I=
joiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ], and using ligh=
t and DNA barcodes to measure biology in context [ https://substack.com/red=
irect/eafbb00a-4e60-46c2-999d-305d8d73e1c8?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7Gn=
A_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ]. This list is non-exhaustive.
A few of our Fund I and II portfolio companies have shut down this year, bu=
t those writedowns are more than outweighed by the markups other portfolio =
companies have received, even in the bear market. Both portfolios, but espe=
cially Fund II, are chock full of breakout and potential breakout companies=
, and the crypto portion of the portfolio is performing particularly well a=
s that market thaws. We tried to back solid projects with real use cases, l=
ike Worldcoin [ https://substack.com/redirect/912deadb-d007-4579-83a3-8bde9=
667cd97?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU =
] and DIMO [ https://substack.com/redirect/8683045b-8510-472a-87cf-2ee5ecc4=
11be?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ] a=
mong others, and it=E2=80=99s starting to pay off. There will be more to co=
me here.=C2=A0
On the team side, working with Elliot [ https://substack.com/redirect/2627f=
754-6a0c-4693-bfd2-a060c7db9830?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjm=
lXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ] and learning about the mind blowing things happen=
ing in bio has been a highlight. Elliot=E2=80=99s combination of science, c=
ode, writing, investing discipline, and ability to dream about the future i=
s incredibly rare, and I fully expect him to be one of the best techbio inv=
estors to ever do it when all is said and done.=C2=A0
I=E2=80=99m incredibly bullish on our strategy as the most important compan=
ies shift from pure software companies to those that combine bits and atoms=
 [ https://substack.com/redirect/146d8ea6-3e3f-4dda-9e70-6aa8ead77db4?j=3De=
yJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ], and as the=
 world shifts from centralization to decentralization [ https://substack.co=
m/redirect/1e039f92-2d91-491e-b4dc-7503c899dccd?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J=
5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ]. We can live in an Age of Miracle=
s, and we want to continue to back the founders who are going to bring it a=
bout. The crazier the better.=C2=A0
We=E2=80=99re still raising (I=E2=80=99ll probably be raising until I die).=
 Get in touch if you want to be a part of it [ https://substack.com/redirec=
t/0a03ec70-db7f-427f-8dc9-616f57d0b2ef?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_Zl=
NuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ].=C2=A0
Optimism. One of the things I=E2=80=99m proudest of is that we planted an o=
ptimistic [ https://substack.com/redirect/1d11b0e9-8d60-4328-9fd6-da3bc54e7=
cec?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ] fl=
ag early and we=E2=80=99ve stuck with it through thick and thin.=C2=A0
As the markets tumbled and wars raged, it was very easy to get views and li=
stens by telling people how awful things were. Pessimism will always sound =
smart and responsible. Showing LPs that you=E2=80=99re being cautious build=
s trust, even if it=E2=80=99s the wrong move at precisely the wrong time.=
=C2=A0
As I wrote in the first ever Not Boring, Not Boring Newsletter #1 [ https:/=
/substack.com/redirect/eda71837-78f7-4f19-a8a2-a6cd01017096?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXA=
wZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ] =E2=80=93 delayed by =
a couple of days because I caught early COVID:=C2=A0
I feel very lucky to get to work with my brother on putting out the Weekly =
Dose of Optimism every week to share the amazing things humans are doing in=
 the world (all while he=E2=80=99s been slinging millions of creatine gummi=
es [ https://substack.com/redirect/6355f8c5-c22d-410f-b448-91ea19081dac?j=
=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ]). We di=
dn=E2=80=99t freak out during the beak market, didn=E2=80=99t tell people t=
he world was ending, recession was coming, or to pivot to AI. We just kept =
publishing the good stuff and stayed true to our conviction that things wou=
ld get better. I=E2=80=99ve heard from a lot of people that they=E2=80=99ve=
 appreciated the optimism in the face of so much doom and gloom.=C2=A0
Now, the techno-optimism is spreading. e/acc is taking off. Marc Andreessen=
 wrote The Techno-Optimist Manifesto [ https://substack.com/redirect/b3119d=
3a-ad8f-4d1d-aab4-1ba5e2bee73b?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjml=
XXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ]. Jason Carman launched S3 News [ https://substack.=
com/redirect/bddfcc68-d76e-4d58-9f9f-9b7593089651?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb=
8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ]. Pirate Wires [ https://substac=
k.com/redirect/62400c52-3b2e-444b-8e32-93c602d2b513?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.=
gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ] is killing it. We love to see=
 it. Optimism shapes the future, and I=E2=80=99m happy to do our small part=
 to spread it.=C2=A0
It was a hard year, but looking back, it was a year of good and necessary c=
onsolidation. We built up assets that are going to be valuable as the momen=
tum returns.=C2=A0
The most valuable asset I=E2=80=99ve built up during the consolidation may =
be the lessons:=C2=A0
Appreciate momentum for what it is. Enjoy it, but know that it=E2=80=99s fi=
ckle. Use the opportunities it provides to build something solid. Take prof=
its sometimes.=C2=A0
Master all aspects of your craft. Focus on getting better at the things you=
 can control even when momentum could carry you so that you=E2=80=99re read=
y when it doesn=E2=80=99t.=C2=A0
Stay Weird. Don=E2=80=99t let mastering your craft make you boring. Use it =
as a platform off of which you can get even weirder. Business in the back, =
party in the front.=C2=A0
Build conviction. If you know why you=E2=80=99re doing what you=E2=80=99re =
doing and why you believe what you believe, bad markets can be great opport=
unities.=C2=A0=C2=A0
Say no and yes more. Say no to the million little things and yes to a few b=
ig things.=C2=A0
Have Agency. Focus on being great at the things you can control.=C2=A0
Both riding momentum and agonizing over its loss are low-agency moves. I ca=
n write better essays, invest more in relationships, and learn to manage No=
t Boring Capital like a fund that=E2=80=99s going to be investing no matter=
 the market. All of that is in my control.=C2=A0
I=E2=80=99m fired up for 2024. Expect fresher essays, even if it means that=
 I write fewer of them. I can=E2=80=99t wait to let you know when I close t=
he full fund, even if it kills me, and to share the stories of the world-be=
nding companies we back from it.=C2=A0
I=E2=80=99m going to take a couple weeks off from writing over the holidays=
 to plan out exactly what all of that looks like, try to get some sleep, an=
d recharge the batteries for a big year ahead.=C2=A0
Not Boring is going to be here for a long time, and when we look back in a =
decade, I have a sneaking suspicion that 2023 is going to look like one of =
our most important years. Momentum is intoxicating but impermanent. Consoli=
dation is critical.=C2=A0
I think that will be the case for a lot of us. So go consolidate for a coup=
le more weeks, rest, reflect, and plan, spend time with family and friends,=
 and get ready to break out in 2024.
Thanks to Dan [ https://substack.com/redirect/65a0ae12-07c1-4dab-b5fa-8d8c9=
31e78e7?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU =
] and Puja [ https://substack.com/redirect/f006ad56-f88d-406b-9c67-0cc3874f=
ad2f?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ] f=
or editing (and putting up with me this year)!
That=E2=80=99s all for today, and the last essay of the year. But don=E2=80=
=99t go anywhere, we=E2=80=99ll be back in your inbox with a Weekly Dose on=
 Friday. Happy holidays!
Thanks for reading,
Packy

ht: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">Look at that dropoff! And I’m not even=
 fun! I barely drink and rarely go to bed after 10pm. </p><p style=3D"=
color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20p=
x 0;">Working tired is always hard, but in a normal job, you get to zombie =
out in meetings or have someone tell you what you need to get done, which y=
ou can do kind of on autopilot. Coming up with creative topics every week a=
nd writing thousands of words about them has not been easy, and I think it =
shows. </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; line-h=
eight: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">There’s this sequence that’s =
almost become a routine in our house. I’ll stay up late Monday night =
and wake up really early on Tuesday morning to finish an essay, often rewri=
ting it entirely from 5am - 8:50am when the narrative structure finally sho=
ws itself. I’ll hit send, tweet about the essay, and then refresh Twi=
tter for a while to see if it’s picking up steam. Puja will ask how t=
he piece is doing, and I’ll say, “Fine. It’s doing OK.&#8=
221; </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; line-hei=
ght: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;"><span>Some of that is just flat momentum. S=
haring Not Boring pieces was novel and fun when it was new, you felt like y=
ou were sharing something that people didn’t know about. That natural=
ly decays over time. Put faux-quantitatively, </span><em>s=3Dq*x/n</em><spa=
n>, or something, where </span><em>s</em><span> is shareability, </span><em=
>q</em><span> is quality, </span><em>x</em><span> is novelty, and </span><e=
m>n</em><span> is the number of your pieces people have read. After you&#82=
17;ve written a bunch, the piece needs to be higher quality or more novel o=
r both for people to share it. That’s exacerbated by the fact that th=
ere’s a ton of great writing on startups out there now, much of which=
 is written by new people with novel perspectives.</span></p><p style=3D"co=
lor: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px =
0;"><span>And if I’m being realistic, I think my writing this year ha=
s been </span><em>fine</em><span>. I’ve written some pieces that I re=
ally like, and that I think will stand up well to the test of time, but I d=
o feel like generally, I’ve lost a little heat on my fastball and hav=
en’t thrown enough screwballs. </span></p><p style=3D"color: rgb=
(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">This=
 isn’t just in my head. I talked to a really perceptive founder the o=
ther day who has been reading Not Boring since the early days, and he asked=
 me how I felt my writing has changed. While I thought about it, he said he=
 had a thought: on the one hand, it had gotten more confident as I’ve=
 learned more, but on the other, it was lacking the wild, fresh ideas that =
I had when I started in 2020. </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); =
font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">You know what? He =
was absolutely right! I’d been thinking the same thing and thought th=
at maybe I was going crazy. It was surprisingly refreshing to hear it from =
someone I respected. </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size=
: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;"><span>I actually like that =
answer a lot more than general decay, because I can do something about it. =
If the bar is higher, I need to up my game. I’m not an LLM. IT’=
S TIME TO </span><a href=3D"https://substack.com/redirect/6c7bb482-c3cf-43b=
c-bf0a-514f0d366d30?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8=
-6m8ExkRNpU" rel style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); text-decoration: underlin=
e;">DIFFERENTIATE</a><span>. </span></p><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64,=
 64); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;"><span>Figura=
tively, what’s kept me up at night is </span><strong>fundraising</str=
ong><span>. </span></p><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: =
16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;"><span>In the beginning of 202=
3, the very first post I wrote, I announced that I was raising </span><a hr=
ef=3D"https://substack.com/redirect/3950f612-c3d4-44ab-877b-ad5260a6364d?j=
=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU" rel styl=
e=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); text-decoration: underline;">Not Boring Capita=
l Fund III</a><span>, a $30 million fund. </span></p><p style=3D"color=
: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;"=
>Almost a year later, I still haven’t closed the fund. We’ve do=
ne rolling closes and have been investing, but we’re nowhere close to=
 the $30 million. </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 1=
6px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">It’s hard to admit, but =
it’s true. Fundraising has been absolutely brutal. </p><p style=
=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0=
 20px 0;">Early on, I had a $5 million commitment drop to $1 million and th=
en drop out completely because of the market. I’ve had multiple peopl=
e commit to invest $2 million in the fund and either back out or simply dis=
appear. Countless smaller commitments have ghosted when it came time to sig=
n and wire. I won’t even try to count the “No”s.</p><p st=
yle=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: =
0 0 20px 0;">If you’ve been following the venture news, you’ve =
probably read that it’s a brutal market for venture fundraising, that=
 it’s particularly brutal for emerging managers, and that it’s =
triply brutal for emerging managers who raised their first funds at the pea=
k in 2021. </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; li=
ne-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">All of that is true, but it isn&#8217=
;t productive and it doesn’t help me sleep at night. </p><p styl=
e=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 =
0 20px 0;"><span>I’m more convinced than I was coming into the year t=
hat the strategy – investing in </span><a href=3D"https://substack.co=
m/redirect/8769f005-6fe2-47b4-adb4-288862e46a4a?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J=
5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU" rel style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64=
); text-decoration: underline;">hard startups</a><span>, mainly crypto, bio=
, and deep tech startups – is the right one, and the right one for No=
t Boring Capital specifically. </span></p><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 6=
4, 64); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">But as a l=
ot of founders have learned over the past year, a good strategy is necessar=
y but not sufficient. You need the capital to execute it. And not having it=
 is fully on me. </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16=
px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">I made the assumption that I&#8=
217;d have enough momentum to raise Fund III quickly, and when that didn&#8=
217;t happen, the process dragged out. The more any fundraising process dra=
gs out, the harder it gets. I’ve never felt like I had less leverage =
or momentum than I have throughout this process. You can’t manufactur=
e momentum. Especially in a market like this, people can smell it. </p=
><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; ma=
rgin: 0 0 20px 0;">What’s kept me up at night isn’t the lack of=
 leverage or the rejections; it’s the fact that there have been great=
 companies and founders that I’ve wanted to invest in but couldn&#821=
7;t because I didn’t run a tight process. While I put a ton of time, =
thought, and effort into our investing strategy, and I think it’s the=
 right one for us, I didn’t put enough into our firm building strateg=
y.</p><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26p=
x; margin: 0 0 20px 0;"><span>On the bright side, limited funds have also e=
nforced a healthy discipline that I might not have deeply understood withou=
t living through this market. We’ve built a very strong early stage p=
ortfolio in Fund III, and a smaller fund means that it’ll be easier t=
o return capital to LPs. I just think that we’re in one of those rare=
 paradigm shift periods, </span><a href=3D"https://substack.com/redirect/78=
c8f55f-458e-49ef-9aa9-6d340a81b86a?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaM=
ZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU" rel style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); text-decor=
ation: underline;">the biggest yet</a><span>, and I would like to be deploy=
ing more aggressively into it. </span></p><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 6=
4, 64); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">Nobody owe=
s you the right to be in market, though. You need to earn it.</p><p style=
=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0=
 20px 0;"><span>There are a bunch of lessons I’ve taken from this pro=
cess that can be boiled down to the idea that to earn the right to manage a=
 fund over the longer term, you need to be good at </span><em>all</em><span=
> aspects of fund management, including and maybe especially having capital=
 to deploy when others aren’t. There’s no venture capital witho=
ut capital. </span></p><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: =
16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;"><span>More generally, </span>=
<strong>when momentum is on your side, people focus on your strengths and f=
orgive your weaknesses. When the momentum stops, they scrutinize the whole =
thing. </strong><span>This is true for stocks, funds, people, and even essa=
ys. </span></p><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; li=
ne-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">That’s good! It’s healthy=
=2E It forces you to get fit, to shore=20=
up weak points, and to tighten up argu=
ments. It forces you to master your craft. </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(=
64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">With =
the benefit of hindsight, I think the move is to use momentum when you have=
 it to build things that last no matter the market or your personal circums=
tances. </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; line-=
height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">Personally, I wish that instead of meeti=
ng a million people, I’d spent more time building strong relationship=
s with a handful of people I admire. Instead of investing in hundreds of co=
mpanies to not miss the winners, I wish that I’d focused more on a sm=
aller number that I think will really matter. Instead of taking the easier =
path of being a non-institutional fund, I wish I’d spent time meeting=
 with institutional LPs learning what they look for long before ever trying=
 to raise from them, and raising my bar to meet theirs. Instead of assuming=
 that I didn’t need to be great at fund administration, I wish that I=
’d spent the time to study the art and science of building an endurin=
g venture firm. </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16p=
x; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">When all doors are open to you, =
it’s important to pick the right doors. </p><p style=3D"color: r=
gb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;"><s=
trong>One of the questions that’s tortured me this year is whether I&=
#8217;ll get another shot at doing those things the way I wish I had with t=
he benefit of hindsight, or whether you only get one shot at a first impres=
sion.</strong><span> That question has kept me up at night, too. </spa=
n></p><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26p=
x; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">The answer I’ve come to is one of the reasons=
 I view this as a year of consolidation and not breakdown: yes, I will, if =
I earn it. </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; li=
ne-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">When I started writing Not Boring, I =
thought that my friends had a secret group chat where they made fun of me f=
or writing a newsletter. They didn’t. The truth is that nobody thinks=
 about you nearly as much as you think about you. </p><p style=3D"colo=
r: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;=
">If you do mediocre work, no one thinks about you. On the rare occasions w=
hen you do great work, people do. You can always earn that right back by do=
ing great work. </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16p=
x; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">Write great essays, invest in gr=
eat companies, and stay alive long enough for them to prove you right (and =
return money to LPs).  </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); fo=
nt-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">I’ve found the=
 thing that I want to do for the rest of my career – investing in and=
 telling the stories of the companies shaping the world – and the fac=
t that I still love it despite the difficulty of this year is a great thing=
 to learn. I’m not going to quit; I’m more motivated to do what=
 it takes to become great at it, momentum or not. </p><p style=3D"colo=
r: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;=
">As Leo said:  </p><div class=3D"captioned-image-container-stati=

 line-height: 1.16em; margin: 1em 0 0.625em 0; position: relative;"><strong=
>Breakout Time</strong></h3><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: =
16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">So to recap, this has been a =
hard year. I think it’s been a hard year for a lot of people. It feel=
s silly to call this hard when there are wars happening; hard is relative. =
I consider myself very lucky that it’s been hard in the ways it&#8217=
;s been hard: getting to write this newsletter, raise a fund, and raise May=
a are all priceless privileges. </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64)=
; font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">Throughout the y=
ear, as I’ve agonized over the loss of momentum, I’ve wondered =
if this is it. Once the momentum slows, is it so over or is it possible to =
be so back? </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; l=
ine-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">If you know me, you know I think it&=
#8217;s possible to be so back, but from inside of 2023 it’s been har=
d to grok that. Thinking about this as a consolidation has clarified it for=
 me. </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; line-hei=
ght: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;"><span>Consolidation can go one of two ways:=
 breakout or breakdown. The momentum isn’t </span><em>guaranteed</em>=
<span> to pick back up, but it’s possible, if you play it right.&#160=
;</span></p><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; line-heigh=
t: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">In writing this piece, and using it as an opp=
ortunity to reflect on this year, I actually think it’s been a really=
 productive consolidation period. I did a lot that I’m proud of, desp=
ite the sleep deprivation and anxiety, and we’re coming out of it wit=
h more assets than we came in with. </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64,=
 64); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;"><strong>Age =
of Miracles.</strong><span> We launched a new podcast, </span><a href=3D"ht=
tps://substack.com/redirect/bce82c94-5dcc-486c-8d7d-591885553f75?j=3DeyJ1Ij=
oiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU" rel style=3D"colo=
r: rgb(64, 64, 64); text-decoration: underline;">Age of Miracles</a><span>,=
 that’s gotten over 100k downloads in its first season. I know a lot =
more about energy, particularly fission and fusion, than I did coming in, a=
nd thinking about energy has improved the way that I think about the rest o=
f the tech landscape. That’s shown up in pieces like </span><em><a hr=
ef=3D"https://substack.com/redirect/78c8f55f-458e-49ef-9aa9-6d340a81b86a?j=
=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU" rel styl=
e=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); text-decoration: underline;">Tech is Going to =
Get Much Bigger</a><span> </span></em><span>and </span><em><a href=3D"https=
://substack.com/redirect/2444e274-f78e-4c9d-ae6b-f974902f4c30?j=3DeyJ1IjoiO=
XAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU" rel style=3D"color: =
rgb(64, 64, 64); text-decoration: underline;">The Morality of Having Kids i=
n a Magical, Maybe Simulated World</a><span>.</span></em><span> </span=
></p><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px=
; margin: 0 0 20px 0;"><span>I got to work with an amazing co-host, </span>=
<a href=3D"https://substack.com/redirect/a76b11f5-dd34-40b3-b1fa-892e69bf21=
cd?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU" rel =
style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); text-decoration: underline;">Julia DeWahl<=
/a><span>, meet incredibly smart people across the energy landscape, includ=
ing founders and investors, and built a strong partnership with </span><a h=
ref=3D"https://substack.com/redirect/a7b87ec0-aba7-43a6-8a13-01f1d3330e37?j=
=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU" rel styl=
e=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); text-decoration: underline;">Turpentine</a><sp=
an>. Creating a narrative podcast was more challenging than I expected it t=
o be – tons of interviews, script writing, recording, pickups –=
 but we got so much better at it as the season went along. I think we can g=
row Age of Miracles into something special for listeners that also benefits=
 the fund as I get smarter on frontier industries.  </span></p><p=
 style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margi=
n: 0 0 20px 0;"><strong>Newsletter.</strong><span> Although I think I lost =
some heat on my fastball and didn’t bring the freshness I’ve br=
ought in years past, I wrote a lot of essays that developed the way that I =
think about the world and the outlook for tech. </span></p><p style=3D=
"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20=
px 0;"><span>Some of my favorites are the two I mentioned above, </span><em=

ust, even if it’s the wrong move at precisely the wrong time. </=
p><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; m=
argin: 0 0 20px 0;"><span>As I wrote in the first ever Not Boring, </span><=
a href=3D"https://substack.com/redirect/eda71837-78f7-4f19-a8a2-a6cd0101709=
6?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU" rel s=
tyle=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); text-decoration: underline;">Not Boring New=
sletter #1</a><span> – delayed by a couple of days because I caught e=
arly COVID: </span></p><div class=3D"captioned-image-container-static"=
 style=3D"font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 32px auto;"><figure s=

gure></div><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; line-height=
: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;"><span>I feel very lucky to get to work with my=
 brother on putting out the Weekly Dose of Optimism every week to share the=
 amazing things humans are doing in the world (all while he’s been sl=
inging millions of </span><a href=3D"https://substack.com/redirect/6355f8c5=
-c22d-410f-b448-91ea19081dac?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXX=
epKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU" rel style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); text-decoration:=
 underline;">creatine gummies</a><span>). We didn’t freak out during =
the beak market, didn’t tell people the world was ending, recession w=
as coming, or to pivot to AI. We just kept publishing the good stuff and st=
ayed true to our conviction that things would get better. I’ve heard =
from a lot of people that they’ve appreciated the optimism in the fac=
e of so much doom and gloom. </span></p><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64,=
 64); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;"><span>Now, t=
he techno-optimism is spreading. e/acc is taking off. Marc Andreessen wrote=
 </span><em><a href=3D"https://substack.com/redirect/b3119d3a-ad8f-4d1d-aab=
4-1ba5e2bee73b?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8E=
xkRNpU" rel style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); text-decoration: underline;">T=
he Techno-Optimist Manifesto</a></em><span>. Jason Carman launched </span><=
a href=3D"https://substack.com/redirect/bddfcc68-d76e-4d58-9f9f-9b759308965=
1?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU" rel s=
tyle=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); text-decoration: underline;">S3 News</a><sp=
an>. </span><a href=3D"https://substack.com/redirect/62400c52-3b2e-444b-8e3=
2-93c602d2b513?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8E=
xkRNpU" rel style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); text-decoration: underline;">P=
irate Wires</a><span> is killing it. We love to see it. Optimism shapes the=
 future, and I’m happy to do our small part to spread it. </span=
></p><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px=
; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">It was a hard year, but looking back, it was a year =
of good and necessary consolidation. We built up assets that are going to b=
e valuable as the momentum returns. </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64,=
 64); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">The most val=
uable asset I’ve built up during the consolidation may be the lessons=
: </p><ol style=3D"margin-top: 0; padding: 0;"><li style=3D"margin: 8p=
x 0 0 32px;"><p style=3D"box-sizing: border-box; color: rgb(64, 64, 64); fo=
nt-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0; margin-bottom: 0; padding-left=
: 4px;"><strong>Appreciate momentum for what it is.</strong><span> Enjoy it=
, but know that it’s fickle. Use the opportunities it provides to bui=
ld something solid. Take profits sometimes. </span></p></li><li style=
=3D"margin: 8px 0 0 32px;"><p style=3D"box-sizing: border-box; color: rgb(6=
4, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0; margin-bottom: 0=
; padding-left: 4px;"><strong>Master all aspects of your craft</strong><spa=
n>. Focus on getting better at the things you can control even when momentu=
m could carry you so that you’re ready when it doesn’t. </=
span></p></li><li style=3D"margin: 8px 0 0 32px;"><p style=3D"box-sizing: b=
order-box; color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; marg=
in: 0; margin-bottom: 0; padding-left: 4px;"><strong>Stay Weird.</strong><s=
pan> Don’t let mastering your craft make you boring. Use it as a plat=
form off of which you can get even weirder. Business in the back, party in =
the front. </span></p></li><li style=3D"margin: 8px 0 0 32px;"><p styl=
e=3D"box-sizing: border-box; color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; line-=
height: 26px; margin: 0; margin-bottom: 0; padding-left: 4px;"><strong>Buil=
d conviction. </strong><span>If you know why you’re doing what you&#8=
217;re doing and why you believe what you believe, bad markets can be great=
 opportunities.  </span></p></li><li style=3D"margin: 8px 0 0 32p=
x;"><p style=3D"box-sizing: border-box; color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: =
16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0; margin-bottom: 0; padding-left: 4px;"><=
strong>Say no and yes more. </strong><span>Say no to the million little thi=
ngs and yes to a few big things. </span></p></li><li style=3D"margin: =
8px 0 0 32px;"><p style=3D"box-sizing: border-box; color: rgb(64, 64, 64); =
font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0; margin-bottom: 0; padding-le=
ft: 4px;"><strong>Have Agency</strong><span>. Focus on being great at the t=
hings you can control. </span></p></li></ol><p style=3D"color: rgb(64,=
 64, 64); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">Both rid=
ing momentum and agonizing over its loss are low-agency moves. I can write =
better essays, invest more in relationships, and learn to manage Not Boring=
 Capital like a fund that’s going to be investing no matter the marke=
t. All of that is in my control. </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64=
); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">I’m fired=
 up for 2024. Expect fresher essays, even if it means that I write fewer of=
 them. I can’t wait to let you know when I close the full fund, even =
if it kills me, and to share the stories of the world-bending companies we =
back from it. </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px;=
 line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">I’m going to take a couple w=
eeks off from writing over the holidays to plan out exactly what all of tha=
t looks like, try to get some sleep, and recharge the batteries for a big y=
ear ahead. </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; li=
ne-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">Not Boring is going to be here for a =
long time, and when we look back in a decade, I have a sneaking suspicion t=
hat 2023 is going to look like one of our most important years. Momentum is=
 intoxicating but impermanent. Consolidation is critical. </p><p style=
=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0=
 20px 0;">I think that will be the case for a lot of us. So go consolidate =
for a couple more weeks, rest, reflect, and plan, spend time with family an=
d friends, and get ready to break out in 2024.</p><div style=3D"font-size: =
16px; line-height: 26px;"><hr style=3D"background: #e0e0e0; border: none; h=
eight: 1px; margin: 32px 0; padding: 0;"></div><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 6=

on: underline;">Puja</a><span> for editing (and putting up with me this yea=
r)!</span></em></p><div style=3D"font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px;"><hr s=
tyle=3D"background: #e0e0e0; border: none; height: 1px; margin: 32px 0; pad=
ding: 0;"></div><p style=3D"color: rgb(64, 64, 64); font-size: 16px; line-h=
eight: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">That’s all for today, and the last =
essay of the year. But don’t go anywhere, we’ll be back in your=
 inbox with a Weekly Dose on Friday. Happy holidays!</p><p style=3D"color: =
